prompt:
You are parsing a product datasheet PDF. Extract technical specifications THOROUGHLY.

Return ONLY valid JSON:
{
  "specs": {"<param>": "<value>"},
  "weight_g": "<number only, e.g. 250>",
  "dimensions_mm": "<LxWxH e.g. 120x80x45>",
  "ean": "<13 digits or empty>",
  "series": "<product family>",
  "certifications": ["CE", "EN54-18", ...]
}

SPECS FIELD — include EVERY concrete technical parameter you can find:
- Electrical: voltage (V), current (A, mA), power (W), frequency (Hz)
- Physical: dimensions LxWxH, weight, housing/enclosure material, color
- Environmental: operating temperature, IP rating, humidity range
- Performance: detection range, sensitivity, response time, output type
- Interface: connector type, cable, mounting type, communication protocol
- Compliance: EN/IEC/UL standards, certifications
- Any numeric values in tables (do not skip tables!)

If datasheet has a "Technical Data" / "Specifications" / "Specifications tabulaire" / "Technische Daten" section — extract EVERY row.

Do NOT skip specs because they seem obvious. Extract literally.

If a value is truly absent, omit the key. Do NOT write "N/A" or "not specified".

Return empty specs {} ONLY if the PDF genuinely has no technical specifications (rare for datasheets).

PT-NTP          CAPABILITY LIST 
PART 1 – GENERAL   
Issue C 
Chapter: 1.1 
Rev.  : 2 
Title     : Introduction   
Dated: 30 August 2018 
 
Page 1 of 1   
 
1.1 
INTRODUCTION 
 
The Capability List (CL) provided herein is part of Approved Maintenance Organization 
Manual (AMO Manual) and is issued in conjunction with the limited rating from the authority 
as mentioned on its operation specification. It covers the power plant and its Line 
Replacement Unit (LRU) accessories components that are eligible for installation on the 
engine models approved under 145D-099 limited engine rating, NDT and specialized 
processes.   
Note:  
LRU accessories component mentioned above is including the engine component with 72 
ATA number, which has independent performance and referring to approved data came from 
engine manufacturer or specific component maintenance manual  
 
Responsibility for monitoring the amendments, updating and development of CL is on the 
Head of Quality Assurance and Safety (QA & S). Revisions to the capability list are made as 
required to accurately reflect the current capability as resulted from capability study process 
which perform by Department Engineering and other related function (Accessories 
Maintenance, NDT, etc). All mechanisms are in accordance with the quality instruction.  
 
The CL will be amended with totally re-issued, for instance in the case of a substantial change 
such as the changes of operation specification. The CL is controlled through issue and 
revision numbering. The first issue of this CL was Issue A, Revision 0. Following revisions 
will be numbered Issue A, Revision 1 followed by Issue A, Revision 2 etc. In the case of a 
substantial change this will be issued as Issue B, Revision 0. The full revision history of this 
CL is documented in Chapter 1.5 – List of issues/revisions.   
 
All changed text, including deleted text, in a revision will be marked with a vertical bar in the 
margin. Minor editorial changes that do not alter the contents of the text remain unmarked. 
 
 
A draft revision (in pdf format) will be sent to DGCA by e-mail. Upon review by NTP’s 
DGCA Inspector there may be further changes needed. Once the revision under review can be 
approved by DGCA, the final version will be sent in hard copy. A new revision can only be 
issued after receipt of the approval letter from DGCA. An advance e-mail approval from 
DGCA also justifies issue of the new revision. 
 
Where requested and approved by the Accountable Manager, copies of the capability list will 
be provided to customers to meet contractual obligations or for other purposes. Unless 
specifically requested, these copies will not be subject to revision service. 
 
Accountable Manager will ensure that NTP may not approve for return to service any article 
for which it is rated unless the maintenance or alteration is performed in accordance with 
applicable technical data approved by the Director General. Accountable Manager will also 
ensure that NTP may not maintain or alter any engine component, or accessory for which it is 
not rated and not cover on this capability list.

PT-NTP          CAPABILITY LIST 
PART 1 – GENERAL   
Issue C 
Chapter: 1.2 
Rev.  : 5 
Title     : Scope of Capabilities List   
Dated: 30 April 2021 
 
Page 3 of  3 
 
LEVEL OF MAINTENANCE 
 
Level of Maintenance of this NTP capability list is categorized as follows: 
 
1. OVERHAUL: Limited to maintenance and preventive maintenance including 
disassembly, cleaning, inspection, repair, assembly and testing in accordance with 
engine manual or component maintenance manual or other DGCA approved 
technical data.  
2. REPAIR: Limited to maintenance and preventive maintenance including cleaning, 
inspection, repair and testing in accordance with engine manual or component 
maintenance manual or other DGCA approved technical data.  
3. BENCH CHECK: Limited to maintenance and preventive maintenance including 
cleaning, inspection and testing in accordance with engine manual or component 
maintenance manual or other DGCA approved technical data.  
4. INSPECTION: Limited to maintenance and preventive maintenance including 
cleaning and inspection in accordance with engine manual or component 
maintenance manual or other DGCA approved technical data.

PT-NTP          CAPABILITY LIST 
PART 2 – LRU ACCESSORIES CAPABILITIES LIST  
Issue : C 
Chapter: 2.1 
 
Rev.  : 3 
Title     : LRU Accessories Capabilities List that are eligible 
for installation on Rolls Royce TAY Engine 
Dated: 01 October 2019 
 
Page 1 of 5 
 
2.1  
LRU ACCESSORIES COMPONENT CAPABILITIES LIST THAT ARE 
ELIGIBLE FOR INSTALLATION ON ROLLS ROYCE TAY ENGINE 
 
For Rating of LRU Accessories OF POWER PLANT – ROLLS ROYCE TAY 650-15 ENGINE 
(Component-B) which refer to Other than Engine Manual, the following note shall be applied: 
1. NTP has capability for P/N stated below. 
About column P/N: When Multiple or P/N stated is followed by – All, means that NTP 
has capability for all P/N in related manual. P/N without –All means limited capability 
to P/N stated. 
 
2. Periodic and/or random self evaluation is performed by Accessories Maintenance 
Manager function and is used as reference for add/delete/changes the applicable P/N  
in this Capability List before approval. 
 
3. The Group of B-Components (GC = Group of LRU Accessories) is divided into : 
 Group F = Fuel System 
 Group O = Oil System 
 Group A = Air System 
 Group E = Electrical / Indicating System
